questions.prompt: Why include a unique `event_id` on each billable event?

choices.text: Idempotency / deduplication. Distributed systems retry on network glitches, queue replays, restart scenarios — the same event may arrive multiple times in the metering pipeline. Without dedup, retries cause double-counting → customer billed twice for one API call → disputes, refunds, credibility damage. With event_id (UUID per event), aggregator checks 'have I processed this ID before?' and drops duplicates. Replaying a day's events after a bug fix is safe — duplicates de-dupe. Reliability of metering is a customer-trust issue; errors are remembered. Idempotency keys make billing pipelines tolerant of the chaos that distributed systems inevitably produce. Same pattern as API idempotency keys — different layer, same principle: assume retries WILL happen; design to be safe under them. Critical for any usage-based pricing where customers will scrutinize bills.

questions.explanation: Idempotency — retries / replays don't double-count. Critical for billing accuracy and customer trust.

questions.prompt: Customer says they didn't use that much. Investigation tool?

choices.text: Audit raw event logs for the billing period. Pull events for the tenant in the disputed window; break down by day, by endpoint, by feature; compare to aggregates; ideally compare to customer's own logs if they track. The audit either confirms your bill is correct (provide the breakdown to customer — usually resolves disputes) or finds a metering bug (fix + credit + apologize). For this to work, maintain raw event log (not just aggregates) for at least 90 days — provides forensic capability. Store cheaply (cold storage, columnar formats, ClickHouse/BigQuery work). Beyond 90 days, sampled events or aggregates suffice. Customers often discover their own bugs through this process ('oh, we had a script in a loop'). Transparency wins long-term: customers who see detailed usage breakdowns trust the billing system more than those given opaque totals. Pair with customer-visible usage dashboards — many disputes self-resolve when customers can see usage in real time.

questions.explanation: Audit raw event logs (kept 90+ days). Provide breakdown to customer; find bugs or confirm correct.
